sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 42.2g. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.

sections.title: Proteins

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 95% DV, zinc at 44% DV, iron at 32% DV stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: pros first. absurd omega-3 density because this should threaten the top of the seeds category. extreme fibre density because another elite seed trait. strong mineral support because very hard to dismiss nutritionally.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides. best used ground/processed appropriately because whole-seed use can reduce payoff. not universally loved because practical downside. can be rough for some because very high fibre is not free for everyone.

closing.summary: Flax Seeds has a genuinely strong overall case, especially absurd omega-3 density and extreme fibre density. the downsides are there, but best used ground/processed appropriately and not universally loved do not outweigh the upside. best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
closing.useCaseNote: Best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
